threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still pause at the bus stop when the 149 is late; locals have perfected the art of waiting without an ounce of impatience.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with Adele. Strangers were singing along; felt like we were in a bizarre musical.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the smell of grilled kebabs mixed with fresh coffee wafted through the street during the Sunday flea market.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find spontaneous techno parties in abandoned buildings, complete with art installations that shift every weekend.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their instinctive flinch when BART’s brakes screech in the Tube—it's not just the noise, it’s the PTSD from endless delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni rider serenading everyone with a ukulele this morning. Only in the TL would you find music mixed with the smell of lunch truck tacos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists miss how truly quiet Gràcia gets after 10 PM. They think it’s always a fiesta, but locals cherish the calm once the bars close.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they stop and stare at 90-degree intersections, trying to figure out which way is which. Locals just glide past.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the trains,' and you look genuinely surprised when they run late on weekends.
Heard a guy on the 59 last night arguing with a pigeon. Claimed it owed him a tenner for a lost bet. Pigeon wasn’t backing down.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a cat casually lounging on a window sill while a street musician played jazz below. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here can you fill up your water bottle at the cute little 'Brunnen' fountains scattered around, while dodging the tram tracks in the middle of the street.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You'll spot a true local if they roll their eyes at tourists waiting at the BART station, blissfully unaware it’s been delayed for the third time this week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Heard a saxophonist jamming on the 21 bus, the perfect soundtrack to the sunset over Market. Only in SF do commutes come with a front-row seat to talent.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think we all love paella. You won’t find it on many Gràcia menus; it’s a beach thing, not a neighborhood staple.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the lack of pedestrian crossings on Carrer Verdi, even when it’s bustling with locals.
